query: fluorescent overhead lighting institutional office interior unflattering harsh
message: anti_aesthetics/lighting: flat lighting — fluorescent institutional interiors, depressing offices/corridors, depthless cold light

response.objective_caption: A carpeted office hallway extends into the distance, lined on one side by a repeating series of tall partition walls with white panels framed by dark vertical edges. Rectangular ceiling lights illuminate the corridor from above, and orange and red accent panels appear above select partitions. At the far end, a green exit sign is visible near an open doorway leading to another section of the building.
response.anti_aesthetic_caption: Intentional shallow depth of field creates progressive blur along the receding partition walls, softening distant architectural details into obscurity. The image depicts a carpeted office corridor lined with repeating tall partitions stretching toward a shadowy vanishing point, where the empty, sterile repetition of institutional white and dark panels under fluorescent ceiling lights evokes an oppressive atmosphere of unease and dread. Orange accent panels and a green exit sign punctuate the otherwise muted environment.
